### File Download URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.0.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieve a URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandbox/{sandbox_id}/files/download

### Description
Gets the URL to download a file from the sandbox.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandbox/{sandbox_id}/files/download

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandbox_id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - The path to the file in the sandbox.
- **opts** (SandboxUrlOpts) - Optional - Download URL options.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **url** (string) - The URL for downloading the file.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "url": "https://e2b-sandbox-files.com/download/path/to/file"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Download File URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.12.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves a URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandbox/{sandboxId}/download

### Description
Generates a pre-signed URL to download a specific file from the sandbox filesystem.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandbox/{sandboxId}/download

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandboxId** (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - The path to the file within the sandbox.
- **fileName** (string) - Optional - The desired name for the downloaded file.
- **expiresIn** (number) - Optional - The expiration time for the download URL in seconds.

### Request Example
```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const downloadUrl = await sandbox.downloadUrl('/app/logs/output.log', { fileName: 'sandbox_output.log' })
console.log(downloadUrl)
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **url** (string) - The URL for downloading the file.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "url": "https://e2b-sandbox-files.s3.amazonaws.com/download?...'"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Download File URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves a pre-signed URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## Download File URL

### Description
Get the URL to download a file from the sandbox.

### Method
`sandbox.downloadUrl(path: string, opts?: SandboxUrlOpts): Promise<string>`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to the file in the sandbox.
- **opts** (SandboxUrlOpts) - Optional - Download URL options.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **string** - URL for downloading file.
```

--------------------------------

### Download File URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.5/sandbox/page.mdx

Generates a URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandbox/{sandboxId}/download?path={path}

### Description
Generates a presigned URL to download a specific file from the sandbox filesystem. The URL is temporary and has an expiration.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandbox/{sandboxId}/download

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandboxId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - The path to the file within the sandbox that you want to download.
- **opts.expiresIn** (number) - Optional - The expiration time for the download URL in seconds.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **url** (string) - The presigned URL for downloading the file.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "url": "https://e2b-storage.com/download?token=..."
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Get File Download URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.3/sandbox/page.mdx

Generates a URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandboxes/{sandboxId}/download

### Description
Generates a pre-signed URL to download a file from the sandbox.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandboxes/{sandboxId}/download

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandboxId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - The path to the file within the sandbox.
- **expiresIn** (number) - Optional - The duration in seconds until the URL expires.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **url** (string) - The URL to download the file.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "url": "https://e2b-public.s3.amazonaws.com/sandbox-123abc/path/to/your/file.txt?X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&..."
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Get Download URL for a File

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves a URL to download a specific file from the sandbox. This function takes the file path as input and returns a string representing the download URL.

```typescript
const downloadUrl = sandbox.downloadUrl('/path/to/file')
```

### Download File URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.7.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves a pre-signed URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandbox/{sandboxId}/download

### Description
Generates a temporary URL to download a specified file from the sandbox.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandbox/{sandboxId}/download

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandboxId** (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - The path to the file within the sandbox.
- **opts.expiresInSec** (number) - Optional - The expiration time of the download URL in seconds.

### Request Example
(No request body for GET request)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **url** (string) - The URL to download the file.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "url": "https://e2b-example-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com/path/to/your/file?AWSAccessKeyId=...&Signature=..."
}
```
```

### Sandbox File Download URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/sandbox/page.mdx

Get a URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandbox/{sandboxId}/downloadUrl

### Description
Retrieves the URL for downloading a specific file from the sandbox.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandbox/{sandboxId}/downloadUrl

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandboxId** (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - The path to the file within the sandbox that you want to download.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **downloadUrl** (string) - The URL to download the specified file.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "downloadUrl": "https://e2b.dev/downloads/sandbox-12345/path/to/your/file.txt"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Download Single File from E2B Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/upload-download-files/page.mdx

Downloads a single file from the E2B Sandbox to your local machine. This function reads the content of a file from a specified absolute path within the sandbox and then writes that content to a local file. It requires the 'fs' module for JavaScript and the 'e2b_code_interpreter' library for Python.

```js
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
// Download file from the sandbox to absolute path '/home/user/my-file'
const content = await sbx.files.read('/home/user/my-file')
// Write file to local path relative to the current working directory
fs.writeFileSync('local/file', content)
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create()
# Download file from the sandbox to absolute path '/home/user/my-file'
content = sbx.files.read('/home/user/my-file')
# Write file to local path relative to the current working directory
with open('local/file', 'w') as file:
    file.write(content)
```

--------------------------------

### Download Multiple Files from E2B Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/upload-download-files/page.mdx

Downloads multiple files individually from the E2B Sandbox to your local machine. Each file is read from its specified absolute path within the sandbox and then written to a corresponding local file. This is achieved by performing separate read and write operations for each file. Requires the 'fs' module for JavaScript and the 'e2b_code_interpreter' library for Python.

```js
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
// Download file A from the sandbox by absolute path '/home/user/my-file-a'
const contentA = await sbx.files.read('/home/user/my-file-a')
// Write file A to local path relative to the current working directory
fs.writeFileSync('local/file/a', contentA)

// Download file B from the sandbox by absolute path '/home/user/my-file-b'
const contentB = await sbx.files.read('/home/user/my-file-b')
// Write file B to local path relative to the current working directory
fs.writeFileSync('local/file/b', contentB)
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create()
# Download file A from the sandbox by absolute path '/home/user/my-file-a'
contentA = sbx.files.read('/home/user/my-file-a')
# Write file A to local path relative to the current working directory
with open('local/file/a', 'w') as file:
    file.write(contentA)

# Download file B from the sandbox by absolute path '/home/user/my-file-b'
contentB = sbx.files.read('/home/user/my-file-b')
# Write file B to local path relative to the current working directory
with open('local/file/b', 'w') as file:
    file.write(contentB)
```

--------------------------------

### Download File URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.3.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves a pre-signed URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## Get Download URL

### Description
Generates a URL that can be used to download a specific file from the sandbox.

### Method
GET (implicitly via SDK)

### Endpoint
N/A (SDK method)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **`path`** (string) - Required - The absolute path to the file within the sandbox that you want to download.

### Request Example
```ts
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const downloadUrl = sandbox.downloadUrl('/path/to/your/file.txt')
console.log(downloadUrl)
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **url** (string) - The URL for downloading the specified file.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "url": "https://e2b-sandbox-files.s3.amazonaws.com/..."
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Get Sandbox File Download URL (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/sandbox/page.mdx

This method retrieves a pre-signed URL for downloading a specific file from the sandbox. The URL can be used to fetch the file content from the sandbox environment. It takes the file path as a parameter and returns the download URL as a string.

```typescript
const downloadUrl = sandbox.downloadUrl('/path/to/your/file')
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox File Download URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieve a URL to download a specific file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandboxes/{sandboxId}/download

### Description
Generates a pre-signed URL to download a file from the sandbox.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandboxes/{sandboxId}/download

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandboxId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - The path to the file within the sandbox.
- **requestTimeoutMs** (number, optional) - The timeout in milliseconds for the request.

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
None

### Response
#### Success Response (200 OK)
- **downloadUrl** (string) - The URL to download the file.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "downloadUrl": "https://e2b-storage.com/download?...
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Get Download URL for a File (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.4/sandbox/page.mdx

This code shows how to retrieve a URL for downloading a specific file from the sandbox. The `downloadUrl` method requires the path to the file within the sandbox and returns a string representing the download URL.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const downloadURL = sandbox.downloadUrl('/home/user/my_file.txt')
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox File Download URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves a URL to download a file from the specified sandbox.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandboxes/{sandboxId}/download

### Description
Get the URL to download a file from the sandbox.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandboxes/{sandboxId}/download

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to the file in the sandbox.
- **opts.download_url_expire_in_sec** (number, Optional) - The expiration time for the download URL in seconds. Defaults to 3600 (1 hour).

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **url** (string) - URL for downloading the file.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "url": "https://e2b-downloads.s3.amazonaws.com/..."
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Download a File from Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.3/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves the URL to download a specific file from the sandbox. This method takes the file path as input and returns a string URL. It's useful for accessing generated or modified files within the sandbox environment.

```typescript
downloadUrl(path: string): string
```
